Ahmed Ali
(929) 786-6553 | ahmedkali841@gmail.com | New York, NY | GitHub | LinkedIn | Portfolio
Data analyst and CS graduate who turns messy data into clear, stakeholder-ready insights. Built SQL/Python pipelines, BI dashboards,
and reporting tools across city operations, fraud detection, and security risk analytics. Recent projects include an NYC 311 dashboard
ranking 30+ agencies by complaint backlog, a Power BI risk dashboard demonstrating potential $50K+/year savings versus comparable
commercial tools, and a fraud-flagging system with audit-ready explanations. Strong in SQL, Excel, Power BI, Tableau, and
communicating findings to non-technical stakeholders.
EDUCATION
Queens College, City University of New York (CUNY), Flushing, NY

Bachelor of Science in Computer Science, Dec 2025

stakeholder communication

PROJECTS
NYC 311 Operations Analytics Dashboard
Python, PostgreSQL, SQLAlchemy, Pandas, Streamlit, Plotly, Socrata API  |  GitHub  |  Live
Jan 2026 – Mar 2026
• Built an analytics dashboard ranking 30+ NYC agencies by complaint resolution time, backlog severity, and service request trends.
• Automated monthly refreshes of up to 200K NYC 311 service requests from the Socrata API into PostgreSQL.
• Modeled a 5-table raw / core / marts schema to support faster dashboard queries and cleaner KPI reporting.
• Identified chronic backlog hidden by healthy median resolution times by ranking agencies on p90 resolution time.
• Delivered a 5-page Streamlit dashboard with maps, KPI scorecards, agency filters, and self-serve reporting views.
Credit Card Fraud Detection System
Jan 2026
Python, scikit-learn, XGBoost, LightGBM, imbalanced-learn, SHAP, MLflow, FastAPI, GitHub Actions, pytest  |  GitHub  |  Live
• Analyzed 284K credit card transactions with 1-in-577 fraud imbalance, flagging 79.6% of fraud cases at 90.7% precision.
• Compared 6 models using precision and recall instead of accuracy to avoid misleading results on rare-event fraud data.
• Tuned the decision threshold to keep false alerts below 10%, balancing analyst workload with fraud detection coverage.
• Engineered transaction-time and amount-based features, with round-dollar transactions emerging as a top fraud signal.
• Deployed a dashboard explaining each fraud flag with SHAP contributors for analyst review and auditability.
Security Risk Analytics Pipeline: ETL & Power BI Dashboard
Nov 2025 – Apr 2026
Python, PostgreSQL, SQLAlchemy, psycopg2, Pandas, REST APIs, Power BI, GitHub Actions, pytest  |  GitHub
• Constructed a daily ETL pipeline scoring 300K+ vulnerabilities from NVD, CISA KEV, and EPSS into a 6-table PostgreSQL schema.
• Designed a composite risk score weighting exploit probability 5x more than severity to prioritize vulnerabilities most likely to be
exploited.
• Developed a 5-page Power BI dashboard with product-level risk rollups, weekly trends, and non-technical stakeholder views.
• Found that KEV-listed CVEs made up only 2% of records but drove 40%+ of high-priority alerts, focusing review on highest-risk
threats.
• Estimated potential $50K+/year savings compared with comparable commercial vulnerability management tools.

Sports Media Inc. | Remote
Data Science Intern
• Profiled 14K+ social comments by sentiment to track Super Bowl engagement trends.
Jul 2024 – Sep 2024
• Automated MLB and NFL survey analysis, cutting reporting time 30% and producing 20 stakeholder decks.
• Standardized GA4 tracking and KPI reporting, saving 15 hours/week and lifting tagging compliance 25%.
CUNY Tech Prep | New York, NY

Data Science Fellow
• Selected as one of fewer than 100 fellows across 11 CUNY senior colleges in a competitive fellowship.
• Constructed an ML resume-job fit platform with weighted scoring: 50% exact, 30% fuzzy, 20% cosine similarity.

DoorDash | Long Island City, NY
Operations Associate
